expectations.text: Articles (a/an/the) are largely absent from prose
expectations.evidence: Articles are present throughout: "The most frequent culprit", "A `Map` or plain object used as a cache", "A closure keeps the entire scope chain alive", "In a long-running service", "The buffer accumulates" — full formal prose, articles not dropped.

expectations.text: Response uses contractions (don't, can't, it's, etc.)
expectations.evidence: "you've ruled out your own code" — 'you've' is a contraction present in the response.

expectations.text: Response uses short synonyms or fragment patterns typical of caveman mode
expectations.evidence: Response is entirely full formal prose: "Every time you call `emitter.on(...)` without a corresponding `removeListener`, you add a listener that holds a reference to its closure and any captured variables." No caveman fragments or shortened synonyms.

expectations.text: Covers at least 2 common causes of Node.js memory leaks
expectations.evidence: Covers 7 causes: event listener accumulation, unbounded caches/global maps, closures capturing large objects, timers and intervals never cleared, forgotten async operations/pending promises, streams not consumed or destroyed, third-party module leaks.

expectations.text: Mentions at least one concrete debugging tool or technique
expectations.evidence: Multiple tools: `--expose-gc` flag with `process.memoryUsage()`, `v8.writeHeapSnapshot()` and Chrome DevTools with `--inspect`, `clinic heap` package from NearForm, Node's MaxListenersExceededWarning.
